title: Making New Friends in a Foreign Country

all.en: When I first arrived in Berlin to study abroad, I felt incredibly lonely and homesick.
all.zh: 當我第一次抵達柏林留學時，我感到無比孤單和想家。

all.en: I knew no one in the city, and my German was still quite basic.
all.zh: 我在這個城市誰也不認識，而且我的德文程度還很基礎。

all.en: During my first week, I forced myself to attend a welcome event for international students.
all.zh: 在第一週，我強迫自己參加了一場給國際學生的迎新活動。

all.en: There, I met Mateo from Spain and Akiko from Japan, who were just as nervous as I was.
all.zh: 在那裡，我認識了來自西班牙的馬泰歐和來自日本的明子，他們和我一樣緊張。

all.en: We bonded immediately over our shared struggles with directions and supermarket signs.
all.zh: 我們因為共同面對的方向感和超市標示困擾，立刻建立了情誼。

all.en: After the event, we exchanged contacts and agreed to explore the city together on weekends.
all.zh: 活動結束後，我們交換了聯絡方式，並約好在週末一起探索這座城市。

all.en: Mateo introduced us to delicious Spanish dishes, while Akiko taught us to make sushi rolls.
all.zh: 馬泰歐讓我們認識了美味的西班牙料理，而明子則教我們做壽司卷。

all.en: In return, I prepared traditional Taiwanese beef noodle soup for them one rainy Sunday.
all.zh: 作為回報，我在一個下雨的星期天為他們煮了傳統的台灣牛肉麵。

all.en: Together, we visited museums, attended language classes, and laughed at our funny mistakes.
all.zh: 我們一起參觀博物館、上語言課，並對彼此有趣的失誤大笑。

all.en: Slowly, this foreign city started to feel like a second home because of them.
all.zh: 漸漸地，因為他們，這個陌生的城市開始有了第二個家的感覺。

all.en: True friendship, I learned, can grow anywhere when people are open and kind.
all.zh: 我學到，當人們敞開心胸並心懷善意時，真正的友誼可以在任何地方滋長。

all.en: As the semester continued, our small group became a regular family of four friends.
all.zh: 隨著學期進行，我們的小團體成為了一個固定的四人朋友家庭。

all.en: A French student named Claire joined us after sharing a long bus ride one evening.
all.zh: 一位名叫克萊兒的法國學生在一個夜晚共乘公車後加入了我們。

all.en: She brought freshly baked croissants every Saturday morning to our shared apartment.
all.zh: 她每個星期六早上都會帶著新鮮出爐的可頌來我們合租的公寓。

all.en: Together we planned a long weekend trip to Prague during the spring break.
all.zh: 我們一起規劃了一場春假時前往布拉格的長週末旅行。

all.en: We took silly group photos on the famous bridge and got lost in narrow streets.
all.zh: 我們在那座著名的橋上拍了傻氣的合照，並在狹窄的巷弄中迷了路。

all.en: Mateo somehow guided us back using only a paper map and confident hand gestures.
all.zh: 馬泰歐不知怎地只靠一張紙地圖和自信的手勢，把我們帶回正確的路。

all.en: On the train back to Berlin, we promised to travel somewhere new every season.
all.zh: 在回柏林的火車上，我們約好每個季節都要去一個新的地方旅行。

all.en: When my German finally improved, I started ordering coffee without nervous pauses.
all.zh: 當我的德文終於進步時，我點咖啡時不再緊張停頓。

all.en: My friends cheered loudly the day I successfully held a full chat with the landlord.
all.zh: 我成功地與房東進行了一場完整對話的那天，朋友們大聲為我歡呼。

all.en: Berlin no longer felt foreign because these wonderful people had made it mine.
all.zh: 柏林不再讓人覺得陌生，因為這些美好的人讓它成了屬於我的城市。

all.en: In my second year in Berlin, I moved into a shared apartment with Mateo and Claire.
all.zh: 我在柏林的第二年，搬進了與馬泰歐和克萊兒同住的合租公寓。

all.en: We painted the kitchen walls bright yellow and hung photos from every trip we had taken.
all.zh: 我們把廚房牆面漆成亮黃色，並掛上我們每一次旅行的照片。

all.en: Akiko visited often and brought fresh sushi ingredients from a small Japanese shop nearby.
all.zh: 明子常常來訪，並從附近一間小日本商店帶來新鮮的壽司食材。

all.en: Together we started hosting monthly dinner parties for newly arrived international students.
all.zh: 我們開始每個月舉辦晚餐派對，款待剛抵達的國際學生。

all.en: I remembered how lonely my first weeks had been, so I made sure every guest felt seen.
all.zh: 我記得自己剛來時的孤單，所以總是用心讓每位客人都感到被看見。

all.en: One quiet student named Liu eventually became one of my closest friends in the city.
all.zh: 一位安靜的學生名叫小劉，後來成了我在這座城市最親近的朋友之一。

all.en: For Lunar New Year, our shared apartment hosted twenty friends from twelve different countries.
all.zh: 農曆新年時，我們的合租公寓招待了來自十二個不同國家的二十位朋友。

all.en: Mateo made paella in a massive pan while Akiko folded hundreds of tiny dumplings.
all.zh: 馬泰歐用一個巨大的平底鍋煮西班牙海鮮飯，明子則包了上百個小巧的水餃。

all.en: Claire decorated the entire living room with red lanterns and handmade paper flowers.
all.zh: 克萊兒用紅燈籠和手作紙花把整個客廳布置得喜氣洋洋。

all.en: That night, I realized Berlin had quietly become the place where my heart truly belonged.
all.zh: 那一晚，我意識到柏林已悄悄成為我心真正歸屬的地方。
